_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average price index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice benchmarked to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, reflecting a 32% increase since 2015. This indicates the overall cost of these staple commodities has risen notably over the period. However, the increase is uneven across items and has been driven more recently rather than steadily over the entire timeframe. The indexed figure helps encapsulate price movements across multiple products into a single summary measure.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have surged the most, up 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice with a 22% rise. Wheat and sugar have shown more modest increases of 7% and 5%, respectively. These price changes largely reflect supply shocks due to weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice and weather-related stress in arabica coffee growing regions—rather than broad-based inflation pressures. As soft commodities, these markets tend to be thin, meaning limited supply changes can cause substantial price volatility.
notes.why: The headline rise in the breakfast basket is primarily driven by a few weather-sensitive crops vulnerable to environmental shocks. Because supply disruptions in these thinly traded soft commodity markets can be prolonged, elevated prices may persist for several years depending on crop recovery and growing conditions. This dynamic emphasizes the sensitivity of select agricultural commodities to climate and disease factors.
